vocabulary.meaning: a marked area of ground on which a sport is played
vocabulary.example: The football pitch was wet and muddy after the heavy rain.

vocabulary.meaning: a prepared course or circuit for racing
vocabulary.example: The athletes lined up at the starting line on the running track.

vocabulary.meaning: an area marked for playing games like tennis, basketball, or badminton
vocabulary.example: We booked a tennis court for two hours this afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning: an area of land on which golf is played, or a route used for races
vocabulary.example: The new golf course has eighteen holes and beautiful views.

vocabulary.meaning: a raised square platform surrounded by ropes in which boxers or wrestlers fight
vocabulary.example: The referee waved both boxers to the center of the ring.

vocabulary.meaning: a large flat area of ice, or a floor for roller-skating
vocabulary.example: They spent the afternoon skating at the local ice rink.

vocabulary.meaning: to be the most successful in a competition, race, or game
vocabulary.example: He trained hard to win the gold medal.

vocabulary.meaning: to defeat someone in a game, competition, or election
vocabulary.example: Our school football team beat their rivals three-nil.

vocabulary.meaning: to win a point or goal in a sport or game
vocabulary.example: He managed to score the winning goal in the final minute of the game.

vocabulary.meaning: a dramatic work intended for performance on a stage or broadcast
vocabulary.example: We went to the theater to watch a new play by Shakespeare.

vocabulary.meaning: an activity played for amusement or competition, according to rules
vocabulary.example: Let's play a game of chess while we wait.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who watches a show, game, or other event, especially in person
vocabulary.example: Thousands of spectators packed the stadium to watch the match.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who watches television or a video screen
vocabulary.example: The television show attracted millions of viewers worldwide.

vocabulary.meaning: an official who watches a game like tennis, cricket, or baseball to enforce the rules
vocabulary.example: The tennis umpire called the ball out.

vocabulary.meaning: an official who supervises a game (such as football or boxing) to make sure players follow rules
vocabulary.example: The referee blew his whistle and gave a penalty.

vocabulary.meaning: the last match, game, or trial in a tournament or competition
vocabulary.example: He made it to the final of the men's singles tennis tournament.

vocabulary.meaning: the last part of a performance, piece of music, or play, especially when dramatic
vocabulary.example: The concert ended with a spectacular finale involving fireworks.

vocabulary.meaning: the final part of a period of time, event, activity, or story
vocabulary.example: At the end of the day, we were all exhausted.

vocabulary.meaning: the last part of a story, film, or play
vocabulary.example: The movie was great, but I didn't like the sad ending.

vocabulary.meaning: an implement used for hitting the ball in games like baseball, cricket, or table tennis
vocabulary.example: He swung his baseball bat and hit a home run.

vocabulary.meaning: a long, thin piece of wood or plastic used to hit the puck or ball in hockey
vocabulary.example: She broke her hockey stick during the match.

vocabulary.meaning: a long, thin, flexible pole used with a line and hook for catching fish
vocabulary.example: He packed his fishing rod and went down to the river.

vocabulary.meaning: an implement with a round or oval frame and tight strings, used in tennis, badminton, or squash
vocabulary.example: I need to buy a new tennis racket before the tournament starts.

vocabulary.meaning: engaging in a pursuit, study, or sport as a pastime rather than a profession
vocabulary.example: He is an amateur photographer who takes pictures in his free time.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to or belonging to a profession, or playing a sport for money
vocabulary.example: She is a professional golfer who competes in international tournaments.

vocabulary.meaning: an activity involving physical exertion and skill in which an individual or team competes
vocabulary.example: Football is the most popular sport in the country.

vocabulary.meaning: the sport of competing in track and field events, including running, jumping, and throwing
vocabulary.example: The school athletics team won three trophies at the championship.

vocabulary.meaning: a short period between the parts of a play, film, concert, or opera
vocabulary.example: During the interval, we went to buy some drinks.

vocabulary.meaning: the short period of rest between the two halves of a sports match
vocabulary.example: The coach gave a motivating speech to the players at half-time.

vocabulary.meaning: to finish a game or competition with an equal score for both sides
vocabulary.example: The match was very close, and they ended up drawing two-two.

vocabulary.meaning: to be the same in quantity, size, degree, value, or status
vocabulary.example: No one has been able to equal his world record in high jump.

vocabulary.meaning: a person or team taking part in a sporting contest or business competition
vocabulary.example: There were over a hundred competitors in the marathon.

vocabulary.meaning: someone who competes against or fights another in a contest, game, or argument
vocabulary.example: He shook hands with his opponent after the boxing match.

vocabulary.meaning: a non-participating observer or spectator of an event
vocabulary.example: A crowd of onlookers gathered at the scene of the accident.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who is present at an event but does not take part in it
vocabulary.example: Innocent bystanders were injured when the fight broke out.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who sees an event, typically a crime or accident, take place
vocabulary.example: The police are looking for witnesses to the bank robbery.

vocabulary.meaning: the group of people gathered to watch a performance, play, or speech
vocabulary.example: The audience clapped enthusiastically at the end of the show.

vocabulary.meaning: a pale yellow oil obtained from flaxseed, used in paint and wood varnishing
vocabulary.example: He applied linseed oil to the wooden table to protect it.

vocabulary.meaning: the period of academic study during the winter season
vocabulary.example: Final exams for the winter term will begin next Monday.

vocabulary.meaning: to twist and injure the joint connecting the foot to the leg
vocabulary.example: She tripped on a rock and managed to sprain her ankle.

vocabulary.meaning: to throw a small cube with faces numbered from one to six to generate a random number
vocabulary.example: You need to roll a dice to determine who goes first.

vocabulary.meaning: a school or college club that participates in formal verbal arguments against other teams
vocabulary.example: He joined the school debating team to practice public speaking.

vocabulary.meaning: a published, broadcast, or recorded work that continues the story or develops the theme of an earlier one
vocabulary.example: The movie was so successful that they are already planning a sequel.

vocabulary.meaning: a small platform on which a person stands to be seen by an audience, or a platform for sports winners
vocabulary.example: The winner stood proudly on the podium to receive the gold medal.

vocabulary.meaning: a small amount of money given regularly to a child by their parents (UK English)
vocabulary.example: He spends his pocket money on sweets and comics.

vocabulary.meaning: a sum of money allotted or granted regularly, e.g., to a child (US English)
vocabulary.example: She gets a weekly allowance of ten dollars for doing household chores.

vocabulary.meaning: a game played on a billiard table with 22 balls, where players use cues to hit balls into pockets
vocabulary.example: They went to the pub to play a game of snooker.

vocabulary.meaning: a commercial venue where people go to play snooker or billiards
vocabulary.example: The old cinema was converted into a modern snooker hall.

vocabulary.meaning: the essential equipment used to play snooker, pool, or billiards
vocabulary.example: The club has high-quality tables and cues for its members.

vocabulary.meaning: to continuously toss and catch several objects in the air at the same time
vocabulary.example: He learned to juggle three tennis balls at a young age.

vocabulary.meaning: a classic board game for two to four players, in which players race their tokens from start to finish
vocabulary.example: They played Ludo together on a rainy Sunday afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning: a children's board game played on a grid, where players climb ladders and slide down snakes
vocabulary.example: Snakes & ladders is a simple and fun game for young children.

vocabulary.meaning: various sports or skills, mainly of East Asian origin, such as karate or judo, used for self-defense
vocabulary.example: He has been practicing martial arts since he was ten years old.

vocabulary.meaning: to protect someone or something from harm or attack
vocabulary.example: The soldiers fought bravely to defend their castle.

vocabulary.meaning: a sport in which two opponents struggle hand-to-hand to pin each other's shoulders to the ground
vocabulary.example: Wrestling requires a lot of physical strength and technique.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: change the date or time of an event so that it happens earlier
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: They had to bring the meeting forward from Friday to Wednesday.
EX: Carry on with your work while I am out of the room.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: finally find the time to do something after planning it for a long time
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I still haven't got round to fixing the broken door.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: do something, especially something you should not be doing
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: What did you get up to over the weekend?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: enter a competition, or have something as an interest or career
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She decided to go in for the national piano competition.
EX: I went off coffee after I drank too much of it last year.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: participate in an activity with other people
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We are playing football, do you want to join in?

phrasalVerbs.meaning: defeat and eliminate a competitor from a tournament, or render unconscious
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Our team was knocked out of the cup in the first round.
EX: Look out! There is a car coming!

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop being involved in an activity or agreement
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The sponsors decided to pull out of the project at the last minute.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: postpone an event or activity to a later time
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Never put off until tomorrow what you can do today.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: tolerate or accept an unpleasant situation or person without complaining
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I don't know how she puts up with his constant complaining.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: begin to do something as a habit, or start to like someone or something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He has taken to going for a run every morning before breakfast.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: start a new hobby, sport, or activity
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She decided to take up painting as a hobby.

collocations.meaning: make the most of a bad situation or enjoy it as much as possible
try as hard as you can to achieve something
be superior to others in a particular activity
your turn to move or act in a game
attempt to do something, especially for the first time
have the opportunity to perform an action
have the likelihood of doing something
have very little probability of doing something
the possibility of you performing an action
take a risk on something in hopes of a favorable outcome
a rare and exceptionally good opportunity
measuring a specific height
scared of being in high places
the distance from the base to the top of something
extremely liking or enthusiastic about something or someone
become insane or extremely angry
find enjoyment or satisfaction in an activity
receive enjoyment from something
liked or approved of by a group of people
on the other side of a street, room, etc.
on the side that is furthest away from you
support one person or group in an argument
being part of the team or group that wins or loses
have a natural ability to do something well
a competition where people show their special abilities
rotate to face the opposite direction or look away
flip something to see the other side
one after another in a regular order
do something alternately with others
do something one after another in order
the appropriate time for you to do something
at the planned time // early enough
continuously during a particular period
past the time when something should have been done
do something slowly and without rushing
spend time in order to do something
something uses a lot of your time
pass time in a particular activity
devote time to a specific task or object
at a particular point in time
time goes by
make time in your schedule to do something
arrange your schedule to allow time for something
temporarily; for now
enjoy doing a particular activity
read the clock to know what time it is
time when you do not have to work or study

wordPatterns.meaning: take part in a contest against someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: He had to compete against the best players in the world.

wordPatterns.meaning: take part in a contest for a prize or in a category
wordPatterns.example: EX: They are competing for the championship cup.

wordPatterns.meaning: focus all of one's attention on something
wordPatterns.example: EX: You need to concentrate on finishing your degree.

wordPatterns.meaning: needing much effort or skill to accomplish
wordPatterns.example: EX: It is difficult to learn a new language as an adult.

wordPatterns.meaning: experience something as hard to do
wordPatterns.example: EX: I find grammar exams very difficult.

wordPatterns.meaning: experience that performing an action is hard
wordPatterns.example: EX: She finds it difficult to wake up early.

wordPatterns.meaning: able to do something without restriction
wordPatterns.example: EX: You are free to leave whenever you want.

wordPatterns.meaning: not containing or affected by something unpleasant
wordPatterns.example: EX: The product is free from artificial colors.

wordPatterns.meaning: available for a particular activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: Are you free for a chat this afternoon?

wordPatterns.meaning: showing curiosity or concern about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: He is interested in learning how to play chess.

wordPatterns.meaning: include something as a necessary part or result
wordPatterns.example: EX: The job involves travelling abroad twice a month.

wordPatterns.meaning: be engaged or participating in an activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: He was involved in organizing the sports event.

wordPatterns.meaning: have a strong affection or liking for something or someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: I love reading books in my free time.

wordPatterns.meaning: very much want to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I would love to travel around the world.

wordPatterns.meaning: intend to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: I didn't mean to hurt your feelings.

wordPatterns.meaning: it implies that a certain situation is true
wordPatterns.example: EX: The red light means that the machine is recording.

wordPatterns.meaning: it involves or results in something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Working here means getting up at five every morning.

wordPatterns.verb: prefer to do / rather than [to] do
wordPatterns.meaning: like one action better than another
wordPatterns.example: EX: I prefer to stay at home rather than go out tonight.

wordPatterns.meaning: like one thing better than another
wordPatterns.example: EX: I prefer tea rather than coffee.

wordPatterns.meaning: like one thing or action better than another
wordPatterns.example: EX: She prefers playing tennis to watching it.

wordPatterns.meaning: interrupt an activity to perform another action
wordPatterns.example: EX: He stopped to tie his shoelaces.

wordPatterns.meaning: cease performing an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: You should stop smoking for your health.

wordPatterns.meaning: prevent someone from performing an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: The security guards stopped him from entering the building.

wordPatterns.meaning: ensure or feel confident that something is true
wordPatterns.example: EX: I wanted to make sure that the doors were locked.

wordPatterns.meaning: bound or destined to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: The project is sure to succeed.

wordPatterns.meaning: feel confident or have no doubt about something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Are you absolutely certain of your facts?

wordFormation.meaning: allow: let someone do something / allowance: amount of money or permission given / disallow: refuse to declare valid / allowable: permitted or acceptable
wordFormation.example:
EX: Parents should not allow children to stay up late.
EX: He gets a monthly allowance from his parents.
EX: The referee disallowed the goal.
EX: What is the maximum allowable weight for luggage?

wordFormation.meaning: associate: connect someone or something in your mind / association: a group of people organized for a joint purpose / disassociate: declare oneself unconnected / associated: connected or related / unassociated: not connected or related
wordFormation.example:
EX: I always associate smell of pine with Christmas.
EX: She is a member of the football association.
EX: He tried to disassociate himself from the scandal.
EX: There are risks associated with this treatment.
EX: These events are completely unassociated with each other.

wordFormation.meaning: enjoy: take pleasure in / enjoyment: state or process of taking pleasure in / enjoyable: giving pleasure
wordFormation.example:
EX: We enjoyed our holiday very much.
EX: The books brought him hours of enjoyment.
EX: We had a very enjoyable evening together.

wordFormation.meaning: maintain: keep something in good condition / maintenance: process of keeping something in good condition
wordFormation.example:
EX: You must maintain a constant speed on the highway.
EX: Car maintenance is essential for safety.

wordFormation.meaning: compete: strive to win against others / competitor: person who takes part in a contest / competition: contest where people strive to win / competitive: relating to or characterized by competition
wordFormation.example:
EX: Professional athletes compete at the highest level.
EX: There are many competitors in the race.
EX: She won first prize in the competition.
EX: He is a very competitive person who hates losing.

wordFormation.meaning: equip: provide with necessary tools or resources / equipment: necessary items for a particular purpose / equipped: provided with necessary items
wordFormation.example:
EX: We need to equip the school with new computers.
EX: They bought new sports equipment.
EX: The kitchen is fully equipped with modern appliances.

wordFormation.meaning: fortune: chance or luck, or a large amount of money / misfortune: bad luck or an unfortunate event / fortunate: having good luck / unfortunate: having bad luck / fortunately: by good luck / unfortunately: by bad luck
wordFormation.example:
EX: He made a fortune in the stock market.
EX: She had the misfortune to break her leg.
EX: I was fortunate to find a job quickly.
EX: It was an unfortunate accident.
EX: Fortunately, the weather cleared up.
EX: Unfortunately, all tickets were sold out.

wordFormation.meaning: interest: feeling of wanting to know about something / interesting: holding attention / uninteresting: boring / interested: showing curiosity / uninterested: not interested
wordFormation.example:
EX: He has a keen interest in history.
EX: The book has a very interesting plot.
EX: The lecture was dry and uninteresting.
EX: Are you interested in joining our club?
EX: He seemed completely uninterested in the discussion.

wordFormation.meaning: know: have information in mind / knowledge: information and skills acquired / acknowledge: accept or admit the truth of / knowledgeable: intelligent and well-informed / unknowledgeable: lacking information or knowledge
wordFormation.example:
EX: Do you know the answer to this question?
EX: He has a wide knowledge of history.
EX: They acknowledged their mistakes.
EX: She is very knowledgeable about classical music.
EX: He is completely unknowledgeable about computers.

wordFormation.meaning: lose: fail to keep or win / loss: fact or process of losing / lost: unable to be found or having failed to win
wordFormation.example:
EX: We don't want to lose this match.
EX: The company reported a huge financial loss.
EX: He got lost in the forest.

wordFormation.meaning: practice: perform an activity repeatedly to improve / practical: relating to action rather than theory / impractical: not sensible or realistic / practically: virtual or in a practical way / impractically: in a way that is not realistic
wordFormation.example:
EX: You need to practice playing scales on the piano.
EX: He gave me some practical advice.
EX: It is impractical to build a road here.
EX: The match is practically over.
EX: The room was decorated impractically.

wordFormation.meaning: medal: metal disc awarded for achievement / medalist: person who wins a medal / medallion: a large medal, usually worn on a necklace
wordFormation.example:
EX: He won a gold medal at the Olympics.
EX: The Olympic medalists stood on the podium.
EX: She wore a gold medallion around her neck.

wordFormation.meaning: train: teach a person or animal a skill / trainer: person who trains people or athletes / retraining: teaching new skills, or process of learning them
wordFormation.example:
EX: She is training to become a pilot.
EX: The athletic trainer helped him warm up.
EX: Retraining is necessary for workers in the digital age.

wordFormation.meaning: oppose: disagree with and try to prevent / opposition: resistance or a group of competitors / opponent: competitor or adversary / opposite: completely different or facing / opposed: completely against / opposing: in conflict or competition
wordFormation.example:
EX: Many local residents oppose the new building plan.
EX: The plan met with strong opposition.
EX: He defeated his opponent in the final round.
EX: They stood on opposite sides of the room.
EX: We are opposed to any form of discrimination.
EX: The two opposing teams shook hands before the match.
